name: Cargo Project Init with Proper Structure
description: Initialize a new Rust project with Cargo, set up workspace structure, and configure essential project metadata.
trendingReason: Rust adoption continues to accelerate for systems, CLI, and WebAssembly projects

steps.title: Create a new binary project
steps.description: Initialize a new Rust project with a binary target using Cargo.
steps.tip: Use 'cargo new --lib mylib' to create a library crate instead of a binary.

steps.title: Set up a workspace for multiple crates
steps.description: Create a Cargo workspace to manage multiple related crates in one repository.
steps.command:
mkdir myproject && cd myproject && cat > Cargo.toml << 'EOF'
[workspace]
members = [
    "crates/core",
    "crates/cli",
    "crates/api",
]
resolver = "2"
EOF
mkdir -p crates && cargo new crates/core --lib && cargo new crates/cli && cargo new crates/api

steps.title: Configure Cargo.toml metadata
steps.description: Add essential metadata to Cargo.toml for documentation and publishing.
steps.command:
cat >> Cargo.toml << 'EOF'

[package]
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.75"
description = "A brief description of the project"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/yourname/myapp"
keywords = ["cli", "tool"]
categories = ["command-line-utilities"]
EOF

steps.title: Add common dependencies
steps.description: Use cargo add to install dependencies with proper version constraints.
steps.command: cargo add serde --features derive && cargo add tokio --features full && cargo add anyhow
steps.tip: cargo add automatically picks the latest compatible version and adds it to Cargo.toml.

steps.title: Build and run the project
steps.description: Verify the setup by building and running the application.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Rust Project Setup
set -euo pipefail

PROJECT=${1:?"Usage: $0 <project-name> [--lib]"}
TYPE=${2:-""}

echo "=== Creating Rust Project: $PROJECT ==="
if [ "$TYPE" = "--lib" ]; then
  cargo new --lib "$PROJECT"
else
  cargo new "$PROJECT"
fi
cd "$PROJECT"

echo ""
echo "=== Project Structure ==="
find . -type f | head -20

echo ""
echo "=== Building ==="
cargo build

echo ""
echo "=== Cargo Commands ==="
echo "Build:     cargo build"
echo "Run:       cargo run"
echo "Test:      cargo test"
echo "Check:     cargo check"
echo "Add dep:   cargo add serde --features derive"
echo "Doc:       cargo doc --open"
echo "Release:   cargo build --release"

faq.question: What is the difference between cargo new and cargo init?
faq.answer: 'cargo new' creates a new directory with the project. 'cargo init' initializes a project in the current directory. Use init when you already have a directory set up.

faq.question: Should I use a workspace for every project?
faq.answer: Workspaces are useful when you have multiple crates that share dependencies. For small single-crate projects, a workspace adds unnecessary complexity.

faq.question: What Rust edition should I use?
faq.answer: Use the latest edition (2021 or newer). Editions are backwards-compatible and unlock new language features. Set it in Cargo.toml under [package].
